compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Evidence for AC3 (external/operational): the v0.7.0 GitHub Release EXISTS and is marked Latest. Verified with 'gh release list --repo navapbc/rebar' → 'v0.7.0  Latest  v0.7.0'. Release URL: https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/releases/tag/v0.7.0 with assets nava_rebar-0.7.0-py3-none-any.whl + nava_rebar-0.7.0.tar.gz attached. Created as a one-off via 'gh release create v0.7.0 --generate-notes --latest' because PyPI 0.7.0 is immutable and the tag workflow cannot be safely re-run. AC1 (GH_REPO fix) merged as change 316; AC2 (next tag release auto-creates) is forward-looking and covered by the merged fix.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Push a v* tag (v0.7.0) → release.yml runs.
2. Build + Publish to PyPI succeed.
3. The `github_release` job fails in ~6s.

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** the job creates the GitHub Release for the tag.
- **Actual:** step fails with `failed to run git: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git` (exit 1). GitHub Actions run 28763401143, job "Create GitHub Release".

## Root cause
The `github_release` job only runs `actions/download-artifact` then `gh release …`. It never runs `actions/checkout`, and sets no `GH_REPO`, so the `gh` CLI has no repository context and tries to infer it from a local git repo that isn't there.

## Fix
Set `GH_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}` in the step env (lighter than a full checkout; `gh release create/view/edit/upload` and `--generate-notes` all work against the API with GH_REPO set). Introduced by change 314 (ticket f112-6222-e236-4b09).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` `github_release` step sets `GH_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}` (or checks out the repo) so `gh` has repo context.
- [ ] The next tag-triggered release creates the GitHub Release automatically without the git-repository error.
- [ ] v0.7.0 GitHub Release exists and is marked Latest (created as a one-off since PyPI 0.7.0 is immutable and the tag workflow cannot be safely re-run).
compiled_state.title: release.yml github_release job fails: gh needs repo context (not a git repository)
